candidates.name: Adobe
candidates.reason: CFO surprise exit + organic ARR guide-down, dual C-suite vacant post-earnings
candidates.thesis: The AI disruption narrative is stale and the valuation at ~11x forward P/E is extreme for Adobe's quality, but the organic ARR guide-down and surprise CFO departure are genuinely fresh information from June 11, and analyst downgrades were still arriving as of June 19 — the repricing cycle is not yet complete. The freemium pivot introduces a 12–18 month uncertainty window with no hard catalyst to end it, unlike the classic capitulation template. Watch for the last analyst cuts to clear, the $190 52-week floor to hold on volume, and any CEO announcement — that would be the specific entry trigger to act on.

candidates.name: SLB
candidates.reason: US-Iran peace deal confirmed; Strait of Hormuz reopening collapses oil war premium
candidates.thesis: This is the second or third leg of a known macro trade — the Iran peace deal and Hormuz reopening were telegraphed for weeks, so this is not first-leg selling. However, oil has not yet found its new equilibrium, sell-side consensus has not capitulated (still overwhelmingly Buy-rated at $59+ targets vs. $48 stock), and Q2 earnings in late July carry real guidance-cut risk. The contrarian case is compelling — a $58B post-war reconstruction cycle and a growing digital segment are being ignored — but the entry point is not yet confirmed. Monitor for: oil price floor establishment, insider buying, or a final Q2 earnings washout in the $43-45 zone.
